title: <name> and the Case of the Sparkly Shoes

pages.text: It was the morning of the school dance, and <name> was so excited! She had a shiny pink dress and her favorite sparkly shoes. But when she opened her cupboard — “Oh no!” she cried. “My sparkly shoes are gone!”
Amma smiled. “Maybe they’re playing hide and seek!” <name> giggled and began to search. “Where did my sparkly shoes go?” she asked her parrot, Mithu. “Check under the bed! Squawk!” said Mithu.

<name> peeked under the bed — only a rolling crayon box. Then she noticed something shiny on the floor — glitter! She followed the trail across the room. “Hmm,” she said, “sparkles, tiny footprints, and… giggles?” She heard a soft “Hee-hee!” from behind the curtain.

<name> pulled it open — and there was her little brother, Aarav, wearing her sparkly shoes! He twirled clumsily. “Look, Didi! I’m a dancing star!” <name> burst out laughing. “You are funny, Aarav! But those are for my show!”

Aarav looked sorry. “I just wanted to try them.” <name> smiled and hugged him. “Next time, we’ll both dance!” She wiped the glitter off the floor, put on her shoes, and twirled in front of the mirror. “Ready!” she said proudly.
Mithu squawked from above, “Case solved! Sparkly shoes found!” Everyone laughed as <name> skipped off to her big dance, her shoes twinkling brighter than ever.
